yes, because "we," as in the Bay area, didn't take a direct hit from Helene. A direct hit storm will bring the worst storm surge south of the eye because that brings the flow inland, where north of the storm is an outward flow. With Helene, the Bay area suffered an unprecedented surge but it still wasn't a direct hit for us. This is serious for our area.
For the area where it makes landfall yeah. Helene was like 100 miles off the coast.

Yes, but with the direction of the storm, surge will be over a smaller area to the south of the center.
For example, if the storm makes landfall north of Tampa Bay, the bay will fill with storm surge and downtown Tampa could well be flooded.
If south of Tampa Bay, the water will be pulled out of the bay and could well empty the bay.
We want a southern landfall.
